title: Assassin’s Creed® IV Black Flag™

notes: Native Steam Client will install the game, and the UPlay client. In one of four tries, the installation may fail to allocate space to download files, but it just need to cancel and try again. The game stops with a black screen, needing to Tab+Shift and force quit the game. As workaround, install Lutris and the Windows version of Steam, in order to install the game script. Once completed, upgrade the wine version to 4.8-ge-faudio-protonfied, to avoid a black screen to start. At least, I can play the game!

title: Assassin’s Creed® IV Black Flag™

notes: Runs great but you need extra wine staging patch. I use the custom Proton https://github.com/GloriousEggroll/proton-ge-custom/releases/tag/4.14-GE-2. To use it extract to $HOME/.steam/root/compatibilitytools.d/ then restart steam. Go to game properties check "force use specifi steam play compatibility tool" and select Proton-4.14-GE-2.
title: Assassin’s Creed® IV Black Flag™

notes: Still black freezes on black screen with 4.11-4 . Works with https://github.com/GloriousEggroll/proton-ge-custom/releases/tag/4.15-GE-4 and adding dxvk.conf in $HOME/.steam/steam/steamapps/common/Assassin's Creed IV Black Flag/ with option d3d11.maxFeatureLevel = 10_1
title: Assassin’s Creed® IV Black Flag™

notes: Runs flawlessly *after* you install a forked version of Proton. Using the report from the user just below me, I added GloriousEggrolls forked 4.15-GE-4 release. Along with D9VK. I had to start the game 3 times or so before it would actually start the game. Once to update Uplay, another to log into Uplay, another for something else, then another and it started the game right away. Also, Uplay will run in the background unless you uncheck "run uplay launcher in background" or whatever its called. Once unchecked, when you exit the game, the launcher will close.
title: Assassin’s Creed® IV Black Flag™
